channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Florida Panthers dominate Toronto Maple Leafs in 6-2 victory
item.description: The Florida Panthers ended their losing streak with a decisive 6-2 win over the Toronto Maple Leafs on Saturday night. Eetu Luostarinen and Mackie Samoskevich each tallied a goal and two assists, whil...

item.title: US-Iran Talks in Islamabad End Without Agreement as Pakistan Mediates Peace Effort
item.description: After almost 21 hours of marathon negotiations under the 'Islamabad Talks,' US Vice President JD Vance announced that Washington and Tehran had failed to reach an agreement despite what he called 'sub...

item.title: Angel Aquino receives recognition for her performance in Taiwanese film 'APRIL' at Asian Pop-Up Cinema
item.description: Veteran Filipino actress Angel Aquino has been recognized for her outstanding performance in the Taiwanese film 'APRIL' at the Asian Pop-Up Cinema, an event that celebrates Asian cinema and its creati...

item.title: US-Iran talks in Islamabad end without agreement, Pakistan praised for mediation
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance left Islamabad on Sunday after talks with Iran failed to reach a deal, according to reports. The trilateral discussions, mediated by Pakistan, lasted nearly 21 hours. Vance ...

item.title: Machakos Governor Condemns Rising Political Violence, Warns of Economic Impact
item.description: Machakos Governor Wavinya Ndeti has issued a stern warning against the escalating political violence plaguing Kenya, particularly ahead of the 2027 General Election. Her remarks come following a recen...

item.title: Genetic Study Seeks to Understand Health Risks in British South Asian Communities
item.description: A new research initiative, Genes & Health, has been launched to investigate health conditions disproportionately affecting British Bangladeshi and Pakistani communities. The study, led by Queen Mary U...

item.title: Tensions in the Makhene family surface ahead of new reality series
item.description: The debut of the reality series 'The Makhenes' on Mzansi Magic has brought long-standing family disputes within the Makhene family into the public eye. The series, fronted by singer and actress Letoya...

item.title: Daniel Bwala Recovers From Throat Surgery After Al Jazeera Interview
item.description: Special Adviser to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Policy Communication, Daniel Bwala, don open up say he undergo throat surgery just eight days after him popular interview with Al Jazeera journalist M...

item.title: Grass fire reported on Mount Asog, authorities monitor situation in Camarines Sur
item.description: A grass fire broke out on the slopes of Mount Asog, located between Iriga City and Buhi in Camarines Sur, on Sunday morning. Local residents first noticed smoke rising from the mountain’s grassy areas...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh rolls out fintech certificate programmes across 68 colleges this academic year
item.description: The state government of Madhya Pradesh has announced the introduction of specialised fintech certificate courses in 68 colleges across the state, beginning this academic session. These courses are des...

item.title: Prince Harry disputes claims by Sentebale charity in High Court defamation case
item.description: Prince Harry has firmly rejected allegations made against him by Sentebale, the African charity he co-founded in 2006 in honour of Princess Diana. The charity, which supports young people living with ...

item.title: US-Iran Talks in Pakistan End Without War Agreement, Vance Reports
item.description: Vice President JD Vance provided an update from Pakistan following extensive negotiations between the United States and Iran, which lasted over 12 hours. The discussions, aimed at reaching a permanent...

item.title: US-Iran peace talks end without agreement after 21 hours
item.description: US Vice President J.D. Vance announced that Iran has declined to accept the terms proposed during a 21-hour negotiation session in Pakistan, marking a historic direct dialogue between the two nations ...

item.title: Impact of Tax and Welfare Policies on the 2026 Scottish Election
item.description: The 2026 Scottish Parliament election, scheduled for Thursday 7 May, is increasingly defined by debates over taxation and social welfare, alongside traditional concerns such as healthcare and educatio...

item.title: Livingstone High Marks 100 Years with Reflective Book Launch
item.description: Livingstone High School in Cape Town marked its 100th anniversary with the launch of a centenary coffee table book that brought together generations of former students, teachers, and families. The eve...

item.title: CAF’s Controversial AFCON Final and the Fate of Referee Ndala
item.description: Patrick Omorodion’s column uses the biblical idea of a 'sacrificial lamb' to describe how Jean-Jacques Ndala Ngambo, a FIFA-listed referee from Congo, was caught in the storm of the controversial 2025...

item.title: Shane Lowry becomes first golfer with multiple Masters holes-in-one
item.description: Shane Lowry has made history at the Masters by becoming the first golfer to record multiple holes-in-one at Augusta National. On Saturday, during his third round, Lowry aced the 190-yard, par-3 sixth ...

item.title: National auction clearance rates hold steady below 60 per cent amid economic uncertainty
item.description: Australia’s housing market continues to face sluggish conditions, with national auction clearance rates holding below 60 per cent for the second week in a row. Over the weekend, the success rate for h...

item.title: Study links ultra-processed ready meals to increased risk of prostate disease in men
item.description: A new study has found that eating ready meals and other ultra-processed foods may heighten men's risk of developing benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH), or enlarged prostate, a common non-cancerous con...

item.title: Arsenal Lose 2-1 to Bournemouth at Emirates as Arteta Expresses Frustration
item.description: Arsenal carry serious wahala after dem lose 2-1 to Bournemouth for their own ground, the Emirates Stadium, on Saturday. Arsenal manager, Mikel Arteta, talk say e be like 'big punch for face' as Bourne...
